name: Enchanted City and Devil's Window Loop from Cuenca
name_original: Ciudad Encantada — circular por Serranía de Cuenca desde Cuenca
description: Cycle a difficult 32.8-mile (52.8 km) route through Serranía de Cuenca, featuring the unique rock formations of Ciudad Encantada.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Serrania Alta offers diverse and captivating landscapes in the northeast of Cuenca, Spain. The region is characterized by dramatic karst topography, featuring elevated plateaus, deep canyons, and river gorges carved by the Júcar, Cuervo, and Escabas rivers. Lush pine forests cover much of the area, providing shaded routes and a sense of immersion in nature. A network of winding roads and tracks traverses this mountainous landscape, part of the Southern Iberian System, offering varied gradients for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Serrania Alta The most popular touring cycling route is Hoz del Júcar y del Huécar — circular desde Cuenca, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic Júcar and Huécar gorges, starting and ending in Cuenca. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Enchanted City and Devil's Window Loop from Cuenca, a difficult 32.8 miles (52.8 km) path. This route passes by the unique rock formations of Ciudad Encantada and offers views from the Ventano del Diablo viewpoint. Local touring cyclists also love the Empty Mountains Route, a 38.7 miles (62.3 km) trail leading through vast pine forests and mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 46 minutes. Touring cycling around Serrania Alta is defined by impressive karst formations, lush pine forests, and deep river gorges. The region offers a variety of routes suitable for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging ascents. The routes in Serrania Alta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Serrania Alta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Serrania Alta?
A: Serrania Alta offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 130 options to explore. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 5 easy, 38 moderate, and 96 difficult paths, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Serrania Alta?
A: The terrain in Serrania Alta is characterized by dramatic karst topography, featuring elevated plateaus, deep canyons, and river gorges carved by the Júcar, Cuervo, and Escabas rivers. You'll also find lush pine forests and mountainous landscapes, part of the Southern Iberian System, offering varied gradients from gentle climbs to challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Serrania Alta?
A: Yes, many routes in Serrania Alta are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079481105/hoz-del-jucar-y-del-huecar-circular-desde-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz del Júcar y del Huécar — circular desde Cuenca&lt;/a&gt; explores the scenic Júcar and Huécar gorges. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1095090297/los-callejones-de-las-majadas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Callejones de Las Majadas Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through unique karst formations.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I visit while cycling in Serrania Alta?
A: Serrania Alta is rich in natural wonders. You can cycle past the unique rock formations of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy breathtaking views from the Ventano del Diablo viewpoint, which is featured on the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1079527955/enchanted-city-and-devil-s-window-loop-from-cuenca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City and Devil's Window Loop from Cuenca&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable spots include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4740317" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Cuervo River&lt;/a&gt; with its stunning waterfalls, and the geological formations at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Callejones of Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Serrania Alta?
A: Serrania Alta is beautiful throughout the year, but autumn is particularly recommended for touring cycling. During this season, the extensive pine forests burst with vibrant greens, yellows, and ochres, offering shaded routes and a truly immersive experience in nature. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Serrania Alta?
A: While many routes in Serrania Alta are moderate to difficult, there are some easier options suitable for families. Look for routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances. The region's diverse landscapes mean even easier paths can offer beautiful scenery without overly challenging terrain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Serrania Alta?
A: Many natural areas in Serrania Alta are dog-friendly, especially on forest tracks and less crowded routes. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations for specific natural parks or protected areas you plan to visit. Ensure your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash where required, especially around wildlife.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Serrania Alta?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 40 reviews. Reviewers often praise the region's impressive karst formations, lush pine forests, and deep river gorges, highlighting the sense of tranquility and immersion in an unspoiled natural environment. The variety of routes for different ability levels is also frequently appreciated.

Q: Are there any gravel touring cycling routes in Serrania Alta?
A: Yes, Serrania Alta offers gravel options for touring cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1087295784/palomera-circular-gravel-route-through-the-hoz-de-huecar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palomera — circular gravel route through the Hoz de Huécar&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate gravel experience. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1094016335/puente-de-las-tres-provincias-circular-gravel-from-the-nacimiento-del-rio-tajo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de las Tres Provincias — circular gravel from the Nacimiento del Río Tajo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes that pass through charming villages in Serrania Alta?
A: Absolutely. Cycling in Serrania Alta allows you to explore picturesque villages that retain their traditional architecture. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366345823/canamares-poyatos-y-santa-maria-del-val-circular-desde-beteta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares, Poyatos y Santa María del Val — circular desde Beteta&lt;/a&gt; will take you through interesting localities such as Beteta and Poyatos, offering glimpses into the region's history and culture.

Q: What is the 'Empty Mountains Route' and what does it offer?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086581860/empty-mountains-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Empty Mountains Route&lt;/a&gt; is a well-known bikepacking route that traverses vast pine forests and mountainous landscapes. It's designed for cyclists seeking disconnection and an immersive experience in solitary territories, offering impressive scenery and a sense of wilderness.
tips: The Hoz del Júcar trail in Cuenca is a very popular and picturesque route. It is an excellent option to disconnect and enjoy nature.🚵🚴🚶‍♀️😎

The Enchanted City, declared a Natural Site of National Interest in 1929. This world-famous geological phenomenon is made up of rock formations sculpted by the action of water, wind and ice, which, by eroding the rocks over centuries, have managed to shape more than curious figures (human silhouettes, objects and animals) with a precision that is difficult to understand, until achieving a city that seems to be asleep under some mysterious spell.

Mirador del Ventano del Diablo is a stunning natural balcony located just outside the town of Villalba de la Sierra, in the province of Cuenca. This viewpoint, carved into the rock, offers spectacular views of the canyon of the Júcar River, whose crystal-clear waters flow towards the city of Cuenca.
The name “Ventano del Diablo” comes from an ancient legend that says that the devil attracted visitors to this place, where they stumbled and fell due to the beauty of the landscape. From the viewpoint, you can observe the elegant flights of the griffon vultures and enjoy the lush vegetation surrounding the river.
It is a popular destination for nature and photography lovers, and is only 25 kilometers from Cuenca, making it an ideal stop if you are exploring the region.😎🚶‍♀️

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: This 52.8-kilometer circular route is estimated to take around 3 hours and 27 minutes to complete. However, this duration can vary based on your cycling pace and any stops you make to enjoy the scenery or visit attractions like the Enchanted City.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ciudad Encantada cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bikes. However, be prepared for significant elevation changes, with approximately 720 meters of ascent and descent. While the path itself is paved, it traverses the rugged landscapes of the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, characterized by dense pine forests and deep canyons.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along this cycling tour?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Enchanted City (Ciudad Encantada), famous for its unique karst rock formations. You'll also cycle through the scenic Trail through the Júcar Gorge and pass by the panoramic Ventano del Diablo viewpoint, offering stunning views over the Júcar River gorge.

faqs.question: Is this touring bicycle route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is rated as 'difficult' and requires 'very good fitness' due to its 52.8-kilometer distance and substantial 720 meters of elevation gain and loss. It is best suited for experienced touring cyclists looking for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Ciudad Encantada cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Cuenca. For parking, you would typically look for public parking facilities within Cuenca or near the starting point of the circular route. Specific trailhead parking for cyclists at Ciudad Encantada itself may be limited, so planning your start from Cuenca is advisable.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca or Ciudad Encantada?
faqs.answer: Cycling through the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca generally does not require a specific permit. However, the Enchanted City (Ciudad Encantada) itself is a privately managed natural site that typically charges an entrance fee for access to its unique rock formations. It's advisable to check their official website for current fees and opening hours before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Serranía de Cuenca region offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, especially in July and August, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Ciudad Encantada cycling route?
faqs.answer: While cycling on public roads and trails within the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca is generally permissible with dogs on a leash, specific rules may apply to attractions like the Enchanted City. It's best to verify their pet policy directly if you plan to enter the paid attraction with your dog.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the [CIMA CU01] Ciudad Encantada * Valdecabras (33% of the route), [CIMA CU01] Ciudad Encantada * Salto de Villalba (26%), and smaller segments of trails like Centro, Bco. Santa Maria y Piedra Yunque, and Ventano del diablo.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, there are small villages and establishments near the Enchanted City and along the main roads where you might find cafes or restaurants. However, it's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's length and difficulty.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead from Cuenca?
faqs.answer: Public bus services connect Cuenca with some of the towns and attractions within the Serranía de Cuenca, including potentially near the Enchanted City. However, options for transporting bicycles on these services might be limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules and policies if you plan to use public transport.
